response.justification: Bogdan is a skilled full-stack web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background and meaningful experience in distributed systems at Sociomantic, but he falls critically short of the minimum qualifications for this role. The most disqualifying gap is C++: the job requires 8 years of C++ experience as a hard minimum, while his resume shows roughly 6 months of professional C++ at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project — with C++ absent entirely from his skills section. His infrastructure work is real but built on DLang and Node.js, not C++ or cloud compute platforms. He has no demonstrable cloud platform engineering depth, no cloud-native architecture ownership, and no technical lead experience in an infrastructure or platform context.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with latency-sensitive DLang services
Consistent staff/senior-level tenure across multiple companies with evidence of mentoring (workshops, code katas, conference talks)
Hands-on cloud tooling experience (AWS CDK at Optilyz) and DevOps practices (CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development)
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months professional (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) against a hard requirement of 8 years — the single biggest disqualifier
No evidence of owning or architecting a cloud compute platform; background is product/application engineering, not infrastructure platform engineering
No technical leadership in a cloud or DevOps context; staff titles appear individual-contributor in nature with no reported direct reports or platform-team ownership
